info.title: Lucene.Net Command Line Utility, Version: 4.8.0-beta00017 commit:[5784b18a4c]

options.name: --encoding
options.description: The file encoding used by the input files. If not supplied, the default value is EUC-JP.

options.name: --normalize
options.description:
Normalize the entries using normalization form KC.
Generates the following set of binary files:
- CharacterDefinition.dat
- ConnectionCosts.dat
- TokenInfoDictionary$buffer.dat
- TokenInfoDictionary$fst.dat
- TokenInfoDictionary$posDict.dat
- TokenInfoDictionary$targetMap.dat
- UnknownDictionary$buffer.dat
- UnknownDictionary$posDict.dat
- UnknownDictionary$targetMap.dat
If these files are placed into a subdirectory of your application named 'kuromoji-data', they will be used automatically by Lucene.Net.Analysis.Kuromoji features such as the JapaneseAnalyzer or JapaneseTokenizer. To use an alternate directory location, put the path in an environment variable named 'kuromoji.data.dir'. The files must be placed in a subdirectory of this location named 'kuromoji-data'.
See this blog post (http://mentaldetritus.blogspot.com/2013/03/compiling-custom-dictionary-for.html) for information about the dictionary format. A sample is available at (https://sourceforge.net/projects/mecab/files/mecab-ipadic/2.7.0-20070801/). The Kuromoji project documentation (https://github.com/atilika/kuromoji) may also be helpful.

arguments.name: FORMAT
arguments.description: The dictionary format. Valid values are IPADIC and UNIDIC. If an invalid value is passed, IPADIC is assumed.

arguments.name: INPUT_DIRECTORY
arguments.description: The directory where the dictionary input files are located.

arguments.name: OUTPUT_DIRECTORY
arguments.description: The directory to put the dictionary output.

options.name: --encoding
options.description: The encoding to use for the stemmer table files. If not supplied, defaults to UTF-8.

arguments.description:
The name of the desired stemming algorithm to use.
<STEMMER_TABLE_FILE>[ <STEMMER_TABLE_FILE_2>...]  The path to a file containing a stemmer table. Multiple values are allowed.

options.name: --update
options.description:
Adds new documents to an existing index. If not supplied, any existing index in the <INDEX_DIRECTORY> will be overwritten.
This demo can be used to learn how to build a Lucene.Net index. After the index is built, you can run the search-files demo to run queries against it.

arguments.name: INDEX_DIRECTORY
arguments.description: The directory of the index.

arguments.name: SOURCE_DIRECTORY
arguments.description: The source directory containing files to index. This operation is recursive.

options.name: --field
options.description: The index field to use in the search. If not supplied, defaults to "contents".

options.name: --repeat
options.description: Repeat the search and time as a benchmark.

options.name: --queries-file
options.description: A file containing the queries to perform.

options.name: --query
options.description: A query to perform.

options.name: --raw
options.description: Output raw format.

options.name: --page-size
options.description:
Hits per page to display.
Run the index-files demo first to create an index to run this command against. You can either use a file containing many queries (each on a single line), a single query on the command line, or omit both options to run queries interactively.

arguments.name: INDEX_DIRECTORY
arguments.description: The directory of the index.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Verbose output.

options.name: --cross-check-term-vectors
options.description: Cross check term vectors.

options.name: --directory-type
options.description: The FSDirectory implementation to use. Defaults to the optimal FSDirectory for your OS platform.

options.name: --segment
options.description:
Only check the specified segment(s). This can be specified multiple times, to check more than one segment, eg --segment _2 --segment _a.
Basic tool to check the health of an index.
As this tool checks every byte in the index, on a large index it can take quite a long time to run.

arguments.name: INDEX_DIRECTORY
arguments.description: The directory of the index. If omitted, it defaults to the current working directory.

arguments.name: INPUT_DIRECTORY
arguments.description: The directory of the index to copy.

arguments.name: OUTPUT_DIRECTORY
arguments.description:
The directory of the destination index.
<SEGMENT>[ <SEGMENT_2>...]  The segments to copy, separated by a space.
This tool does file-level copying of segments files. This means it's unable to split apart a single segment into multiple segments. For example if your index is a single segment, this tool won't help. Also, it does basic file-level copying (using a simple FileStream) so it will not work with non FSDirectory Directory implementations.

arguments.name: INDEX_DIRECTORY
arguments.description:
The directory of the index.
<SEGMENT>[ <SEGMENT_2>...]  The segments to delete, separated by a space.
You can easily accidentally remove segments from your index so be careful! Always make a backup of your index first.

options.name: --directory-type
options.description:
The FSDirectory implementation to use. Defaults to the optimal FSDirectory for your OS platform.
Extracts `.cfs` compound files (that were created using the CompoundFileDirectory from Lucene.Net.Misc) to the current working directory.
In order to make the extracted version of the index work, you have to copy the segments file from the compound index into the directory where the extracted files are stored.

arguments.name: CFS_FILE_NAME
arguments.description: The .cfs compound file containing words to parse.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Verbose output.

options.name: --cross-check-term-vectors
options.description: Cross check term vectors.

options.name: --directory-type
options.description: The FSDirectory implementation to use. Defaults to the optimal FSDirectory for your OS platform.

options.name: --dry-run
options.description:
Doesn't change the index, but reports any actions that would be taken if this option were not supplied.
Basic tool to check and fix the health of an index and write a new segments file that removes reference to problematic segments.
As this tool checks every byte in the index, on a large index it can take quite a long time to run.
WARNING: This command should only be used on an emergency basis as it will cause documents (perhaps many) to be permanently removed from the index. Always make a backup copy of your index before running this! Do not run this tool on an index that is actively being written to. You have been warned!

arguments.name: INDEX_DIRECTORY
arguments.description: The directory of the index. If omitted, it defaults to the current working directory.

options.name: --directory-type
options.description:
The FSDirectory implementation to use. Defaults to the optimal FSDirectory for your OS platform.
Prints the filename and size of each file within a given `.cfs` compound file. The .cfs compound file format is created using the CompoundFileDirectory from Lucene.Net.Misc.

arguments.name: CFS_FILE_NAME
arguments.description: The .cfs compound file containing words to parse.

options.name: --field
options.description: The field to consider. If omitted, considers all fields.

arguments.name: INDEX_DIRECTORY

Gets document frequency and total number of occurrences (sum of the term frequency for each document) of a term.

arguments.name: OUTPUT_DIRECTORY
arguments.description:
The output directory to merge the input indexes into.
<INPUT_DIRECTORY_1> <INPUT_DIRECTORY_2>[ <INPUT_DIRECTORY_3>...]  Two or more source index directories separated by a space.

options.name: --number-of-parts
options.description: The number of parts (output indices) to produce. If omitted, defaults to 2.

options.name: --sequential
options.description: Sequential doc-id range split (default is round-robin).

arguments.name: OUTPUT_DIRECTORY
arguments.description:
Path to output directory to contain partial indexes.
<INPUT_DIRECTORY>[ <INPUT_DIRECTORY_2>...]  The path of the source index, which can have deletions and can have multiple segments (or multiple readers). Multiple values can be supplied separated by a space.

options.name: --delete-prior-commits
options.description: Deletes prior commits.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Verbose output.

options.name: --directory-type
options.description:
The FSDirectory implementation to use. Defaults to the optimal FSDirectory for your OS platform.
This tool keeps only the last commit in an index; for this reason, if the incoming index has more than one commit, the tool refuses to run by default. Specify --delete-prior-commits to override this, allowing the tool to delete all but the last commit.
Specify an FSDirectory implementation through the --directory-type option to force its use. If not qualified by an AssemblyName, the Lucene.Net.dll assembly will be used.
WARNING: This tool may reorder document IDs! Be sure to make a backup of your index before you use this. Also, ensure you are using the correct version of this utility to match your application's version of Lucene.Net. This operation cannot be reversed.

arguments.name: INDEX_DIRECTORY
arguments.description: The directory of the index. If omitted, it defaults to the current working directory.

arguments.name: ID
arguments.description: An integer from 0 - 255 (should be unique for test process).

arguments.name: VERIFIER_HOST
arguments.description: Hostname or IP address that verify-server is listening on.

arguments.name: VERIFIER_PORT
arguments.description: Port that verify-server is listening on.

arguments.name: LOCK_FACTORY_TYPE
arguments.description: The primary LockFactory implementation that we will use.

arguments.name: LOCK_DIRECTORY
arguments.description: The path to the lock directory (only utilized if LOCK_FACTORY_TYPE is set to SimpleFSLockFactory or NativeFSLockFactory).

arguments.name: SLEEP_TIME_MS
arguments.description: Milliseconds to pause between each lock obtain/release.

arguments.name: TRIES
arguments.description:
Number of locking tries.
You should run multiple instances of this process, each with its own unique ID, and each pointing to the same lock directory, to verify that locking is working correctly. Make sure you are first running verify-server.

arguments.name: IP_HOSTNAME
arguments.description: Hostname or IP address that verify-server will listen on.

arguments.name: MAX_CLIENTS
arguments.description: The maximum number of connected clients.
